title: Angle of incidence
short: The angle between an incoming ray and the normal to a surface, measured in degrees.
full:
The angle of incidence θ₁ is measured between the incoming ray and the normal — the line perpendicular to the surface at the point where the ray strikes — not the surface itself. A ray hitting a surface head-on has an angle of incidence of 0°.

It is the input to Snell's law, n₁·sin θ₁ = n₂·sin θ₂, which predicts the angle of refraction on the far side.

It is measured in degrees (or radians).
